mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals a sudden deterioration in labor market conditions, triggering recession fears that drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce a positive mean 60-day forward return with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production signals resilient supply-side capacity that offsets rate hike headwinds, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In tightening regimes, rising INDPRO is followed by negative 5-day returns in > 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 290, persistent inflationary pressure forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL > 290 is followed by positive 20-day returns in > 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls and a decrease in the unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative mean 60-day forward return or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic output, which directly increases corporate profit expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rising GDPC1 is followed by negative 20-day returns in > 55% of cases, or Rank IC remains below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals a sharp contraction in manufacturing activity, triggering risk-off sentiment and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the production drop.
